Tests if the prototype of an object is extensible using `Object.isExtensible`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion that checks if the prototype of a newly created object is extensible. It uses the `Object.getPrototypeOf` method to obtain the prototype of an object created with `Object()`, and then it uses `Object.isExtensible` to verify if this prototype can have new properties added to it. The `assert` function is used to ensure that the condition is true, which is important for validating assumptions about object behavior in JavaScript. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript object extensibility.
